evidence_excerpt: Follow-up up to age 3 years: phenotype restricted to hypopigmentation; 'He is now 3 yr old and did not have any problems except food allergy.' No neurologic or immune complications reported.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing mild atopic features: 'During follow-up, he had mild eczema, mild eosinophilia, and high serum IgE levels... He is now 3 yr old and did not have any problems except food allergy.'

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Initial respiratory/allergic issues earlier in infancy; by age 3 years course stable with 'no problems except food allergy,' indicating improvement of prior wheezing/pneumonia and severe milk reaction.

notes_on_uncertainty: Index time approximated as the documented GS diagnosis at referral to Pediatric Immunology at 8 months. The report includes another unrelated SLE case; events before 8 months were excluded per instruction. Long-term details are limited but indicate stable hypopigmentation with mild atopic features and normal development.
